context: Grandma's birthday cake with blue frosting. We were laughing so hard because Uncle Ravi accidentally sat on the streamers and got them stuck to his pants.
First day at college. I was so nervous walking into the hostel with my heavy suitcases. Met Arun, my roommate, who was already blast music and offered me a samosa.
Family road trip to the mountains. The car broke down near a small stream, and we ended up having a picnic while Dad tried to fix the engine. The air was so crisp.
Late-night coding session for the hackathon. We were on our fifth cup of coffee, and the code finally compiled at 3 AM. The feeling of success was unmatched.
Diwali at grandparents' house. The entire courtyard was filled with diyas. The smell of sweets and the sound of crackers in the distance felt like home.
Puppy Bruno's first day home. He was so small he could fit in a shoebox. He spent the whole afternoon chasing his own tail on the rug.
Graduation ceremony. Throwing our caps in the air under the bright sun. Mom was crying, and Dad was taking blurry photos from the third row.
Rainy afternoon board games. The power went out, so we played Ludo by candlelight. It was the quietest and coziest the house had ever been.
Cooking disaster. We tried to make pasta from scratch, but it turned into a giant dough ball. We gave up and ordered pizza, eating it on the floor.
Sunrise hike on a school trip. We reached the peak just as the sky turned orange. The valley below was covered in mist, and everything was silent.
Sister's wedding sangeet rehearsal. Everyone was tripping over their own feet trying to learn the dance steps. It was chaotic but so much fun.
Losing in the cricket match finals. We were disappointed, but we sat on the grass after the game and talked about all the great moments of the season.
Mom teaching me to make the perfect chai. She said the secret is in how long you let the tea leaves simmer with the ginger.
Surprise birthday party thrown by my friends. I walked in thinking it was just a regular study night. I was completely overwhelmed by the love.
Quiet evening on the terrace watching stars with Dad. He told me stories about the constellations and how he used to watch them when he was a kid.
